item.title: Those who have cut off all fetters, Subdued, liberated, unshaken, without longing — At the proper time offer them the sacrifice. The brahmin seeking merit,…
item.description:
Those who have cut off all fetters,
Subdued, liberated, unshaken, without longing —
At the proper time offer them the sacrifice.
The brahmin seeking merit, give thus.

Those who have destroyed greed, hatred, and delusion,
With taints destroyed, the holy life fulfilled —
At the proper time offer them the sacrifice.
The brahmin seeking merit, give thus.

From: Sutta Nipāta 3.5
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Just as, bhikkhus, a dung-beetle, feeding on dung, full of dung, stuffed with dung, sees a great pile of dung before it. It despises other…
item.description:
Just as, bhikkhus, a dung-beetle, feeding on dung, full of dung, stuffed with dung, sees a great pile of dung before it. It despises other dung-beetles and says: 'I feed on dung, I am full of dung, I am stuffed with dung, and before me is this great pile of dung.'

So too, bhikkhus, a certain bhikkhu, overcome by gains, honor, and fame, his mind obsessed, in the morning puts on his robe, takes his bowl, and enters the village or town for alms. There he eats and drinks to satiety, and is invited again for the next day, and his bowl is filled.

He goes to the monastery and boasts among the Saṅgha of bhikkhus: 'I have eaten and drunk to satiety, I am invited again for tomorrow, and my bowl is full. I receive requisites such as robes, almsfood, lodging, and medicines. But these other bhikkhus, of little merit and influence, do not receive requisites such as robes, almsfood, lodging, and medicines.'

Overcome by gains, honor, and fame, his mind obsessed, he despises other virtuous bhikkhus. This will bring that fool long-term harm and suffering.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 17.5
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Possessing these eight qualities, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu is not loved, liked, respected, or emulated by his fellow celibates. What eight? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu praises…
item.description:
Possessing these eight qualities, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu is not loved, liked, respected, or emulated by his fellow celibates. What eight? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu praises those who are not liked, criticizes those who are liked, desires gain, desires honor, has no moral shame, has no moral dread, has evil desires, and has wrong view. Possessing these eight qualities, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu is not loved, liked, respected, or emulated by his fellow celibates. Possessing these eight qualities, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu is loved, liked, respected, and emulated by his fellow celibates. And, bhikkhus, what eight? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu does not praise those who are not liked, does not criticize those who are liked, does not desire gain, does not desire honor, has moral shame, has moral dread, has few desires, and has right view. Possessing these eight qualities, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu is loved, liked, respected, and emulated by his fellow celibates.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 8.3
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Here, Mahānāma, a noble disciple is learned, remembers what he has learned, and stores up what he has learned. Those teachings that are good in…
item.description:
Here, Mahānāma, a noble disciple is learned, remembers what he has learned, and stores up what he has learned. Those teachings that are good in the beginning, good in the middle, good in the end, with full meaning and phrasing, proclaiming the holy life completely perfect and pure — such teachings he has learned much, retained, recited verbally, mentally investigated, and well penetrated by right view.

From: Majjhima Nikāya 53 – Sekhasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: The Blessed One: Abandoning the five strands of sensual desire, pleasant and agreeable, With faith, go forth, make an end of suffering. Associate with good…
item.description:
The Blessed One:
Abandoning the five strands of sensual desire, pleasant and agreeable,
With faith, go forth, make an end of suffering.

Associate with good friends, dwell in a remote lodging,
Secluded, quiet. Moderate in food,
Robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines —
Do not have craving for these things; do not return to the world.

Restrained in the Pātimokkha, guarded in the five senses,
Practice mindfulness of the body, live full of disenchantment.

Avoid the sign of the beautiful connected with passion;
Develop a mind unattached to the foul, concentrated, one-pointed.

From: Sutta Nipāta 2.11
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, there are these three perfections. What three? Perfection in virtue, perfection in mind, perfection in view. And what, bhikkhus, is perfection in virtue? Here,…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, there are these three perfections. What three? Perfection in virtue, perfection in mind, perfection in view. And what, bhikkhus, is perfection in virtue? Here, bhikkhus, a person abstains from killing living beings, abstains from taking what is not given, abstains from sexual misconduct, abstains from false speech, abstains from divisive speech, abstains from harsh speech, abstains from idle chatter. This, bhikkhus, is called perfection in virtue. And what, bhikkhus, is perfection in mind? Here, bhikkhus, a person is not covetous and has a mind without ill-will. This, bhikkhus, is called perfection in mind. And what, bhikkhus, is perfection in view? Here, bhikkhus, a person holds right view, an undistorted view: 'There is giving, sacrifice, offering; there is fruit and result of good and bad actions; there is this world and the next world; there is mother, father; there are spontaneously reborn beings; there are samaṇas and brahmins in the world who, having practiced rightly, have realized and declared this world and the next by their own direct knowledge.' This, bhikkhus, is called perfection in view. Due to perfection in virtue ... due to perfection in mind ... due to perfection in view, bhikkhus, beings, after death, with the breakup of the body, are reborn in a good destination, a heavenly world. These, bhikkhus, are the three perfections.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 3.117
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Elder Sārabhaṅga: Breaking reeds with my hands, I made my own hut. Therefore, my common name was 'Reed-breaker'. Now it is no longer suitable to…
item.description:
Elder Sārabhaṅga:

Breaking reeds with my hands, I made my own hut.
Therefore, my common name was 'Reed-breaker'.

Now it is no longer suitable to break reeds with my own hands,
Following the teaching of the one named Gotama.

Previously, Sārabhaṅga had never seen the entire disease completely and fully.
Now that disease is seen through the word of the supreme one.

Without craving, without clinging, the seven Buddhas attained Nibbāna.
This Dhamma is taught by those who have realized the Dhamma.

Out of compassion for beings, the four truths are taught:
Suffering, the origin of suffering, the path,
Cessation, the end of suffering.

In continuous existence, suffering rolls on without end.
When this body is destroyed, when life ends,
There is no other rebirth. I am truly well liberated,
Liberated from all bases.

From: Theragāthā 7.5
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: And, friends, what is yearning for the future? 'My eye in the future will be such and such, forms will be such and such, and…
item.description:
And, friends, what is yearning for the future? 'My eye in the future will be such and such, forms will be such and such, and he directs his mind to obtain what is not yet obtained.' Because of that directing of mind, he delights in that. Delighting in that, he yearns for the future. 'My ear in the future will be such and such, sounds will be such and such... My nose in the future will be such and such, odors will be such and such... My tongue in the future will be such and such, flavors will be such and such... My body in the future will be such and such, tangibles will be such and such... My mind in the future will be such and such, mental objects will be such and such, and he directs his mind to obtain what is not yet obtained.' Because of that directing of mind, he delights in that. Delighting in that, he yearns for the future. Thus, friends, is yearning for the future.

From: Majjhima Nikāya 133 – Mahākaccānabhaddekarattasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Having known all abodes, not longing for any, That sage is truly a sage, without greed, without clinging, No longer striving, having reached the far…
item.description:
Having known all abodes, not longing for any,
That sage is truly a sage, without greed, without clinging,
No longer striving, having reached the far shore.

All-conquering, all-knowing, of perfect wisdom,
Undefiled regarding all things, abandoning all,
With craving destroyed, liberated —
The wise know that one as a sage.

Endowed with the power of wisdom, virtuous, observant,
Calm, delighting in jhāna, mindful,
Freed from all bonds, without taints —
The wise know that one as a sage.

From: Sutta Nipāta 1.12
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Elder Son of Jambugāmika: Are you satisfied with the robe you wear? Are you delighted with adornments? This fragrance emanates from your virtue, Not from…
item.description:
Elder Son of Jambugāmika:

Are you satisfied with the robe you wear?
Are you delighted with adornments?
This fragrance emanates from your virtue,
Not from others producing such a scent!

From: Theragāthā 1.28
📖 Read this sutta
